document_title: Diploma in Teacher Education - Mathematics Curriculum

sub_strands.name: 1.1 Whole Numbers
specific_learning_outcomes: Read and write whole numbers up to billions
Identify the place value of digits in whole numbers
Perform the four basic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) on whole numbers
Apply the order of operations (BODMAS/PEMDAS)
suggested_learning_experiences: Use place value charts and abacuses to represent large numbers.
Practice multi-digit multiplication and long division.
Solve real-world problems involving whole numbers.

sub_strands.name: 1.2 Fractions
specific_learning_outcomes: Define different types of fractions (proper, improper, mixed)
Find equivalent fractions and simplify fractions
Compare and order fractions
Perform addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of fractions
suggested_learning_experiences: Use fraction bars and circles to visualize and compare fractions.
Find common denominators to add and subtract fractions.
Solve practical problems involving fractions, such as in recipes.

sub_strands.name: 1.3 Decimals
specific_learning_outcomes: Read and write decimals up to thousandths
Convert fractions to decimals and vice versa
Perform the four basic operations on decimals
Round decimals to a given number of decimal places
suggested_learning_experiences: Use decimal grids to represent decimal numbers.
Practice aligning decimal points when adding and subtracting.
Solve problems involving money and measurements.

sub_strands.name: 2.1 Length
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify standard units of length (mm, cm, m, km)
Convert between units of length
Calculate the perimeter of regular and irregular shapes
suggested_learning_experiences: Measure various objects in the classroom using rulers and tape measures.
Solve problems involving distance and perimeter.
Estimate lengths and then measure to check accuracy.
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 2.2 Area
specific_learning_outcomes: Define area
Calculate the area of squares, rectangles, and triangles
Find the area of composite shapes
suggested_learning_experiences: Use grid paper to count squares and find the area of shapes.
Apply formulas to calculate the area of different polygons.
Solve real-world problems, such as finding the area of a room to be carpeted.

sub_strands.name: 2.3 Volume and Capacity
specific_learning_outcomes: Define volume and capacity
Calculate the volume of cubes and cuboids
Convert between units of capacity (ml, l)
suggested_learning_experiences: Build cubes and cuboids using unit blocks to understand volume.
Use measuring jugs and cylinders to measure the capacity of different containers.
Solve problems comparing volume and capacity.

sub_strands.name: 2.4 Time
specific_learning_outcomes: Read and write time in 12-hour and 24-hour formats
Convert between units of time (seconds, minutes, hours)
Solve problems involving time, speed, and distance
suggested_learning_experiences: Use analog and digital clocks to practice telling time.
Calculate durations of events and create timetables.
Solve word problems involving travel time and speed.

sub_strands.name: 3.1 Angles
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify different types of angles (acute, obtuse, right, reflex)
Measure and draw angles using a protractor
Calculate angles on a straight line, at a point, and in a triangle
suggested_learning_experiences: Use a protractor to measure angles in the classroom environment.
Draw shapes with specified angles.
Solve problems to find unknown angles in diagrams.

sub_strands.name: 3.2 2D and 3D Shapes
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify and name common 2D shapes (polygons) and 3D shapes (polyhedra)
Describe the properties of shapes (sides, vertices, faces, edges)
Construct nets of 3D shapes
suggested_learning_experiences: Sort and classify a collection of 2D and 3D shapes.
Build skeletons of 3D shapes using straws and connectors.
Open up boxes to see their nets and then construct 3D shapes from paper nets.

sub_strands.name: 4.1 Algebraic Expressions
specific_learning_outcomes: Use letters to represent unknown numbers
Form and simplify simple algebraic expressions
Substitute values into expressions and evaluate them
suggested_learning_experiences: Translate word problems into algebraic expressions.
Practice collecting like terms to simplify expressions.
Play 'function machine' games to practice substitution.

sub_strands.name: 4.2 Equations
specific_learning_outcomes: Form and solve simple linear equations
Use equations to solve word problems
suggested_learning_experiences: Use the balancing method to solve equations.
Check solutions by substituting them back into the original equation.
Create and solve equations based on real-life scenarios.

sub_strands.name: 5.1 Concrete-Pictorial-Abstract (CPA) Approach
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the principles of the CPA approach in teaching mathematics
Design a lesson that moves from concrete manipulatives to pictorial representations to abstract symbols
suggested_learning_experiences: Teach addition using blocks (concrete), then drawing blocks (pictorial), then the number sentence (abstract).
Analyse lesson plans that effectively use the CPA model.
Deliver a micro-lesson using the CPA approach for a chosen math topic.

sub_strands.name: 5.2 Problem-Solving in Mathematics
specific_learning_outcomes: Teach problem-solving heuristics (e.g., draw a diagram, look for a pattern, work backwards)
Facilitate a problem-based learning environment
Encourage perseverance and mathematical reasoning
suggested_learning_experiences: Present non-routine problems for students to tackle in groups.
Model different problem-solving strategies.
Encourage students to explain their thinking and justify their answers.
